page: Amazon Web Services m8ine.8xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features a dedicated x86_64 Intel processor running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The hardware configuration provides 32 virtual CPUs derived from 16 physical cores with 2 threads per core. System memory totals 128.0 GB, which translates to a resource density of 4.0 GB of RAM per physical core.

This instance does not include local NVMe SSD storage, meaning all storage operations rely on external Amazon Elastic Block Store volumes. It is network and EBS optimized, delivering a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. The absence of local storage represents a tradeoff, requiring network-attached storage for persistent data, but the high baseline bandwidth helps mitigate potential latency. No dedicated GPU hardware is present on this instance.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m8ine.8xlarge offers a balanced resource allocation profile, combining dedicated compute resources with a standard 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. This configuration is designed for workloads requiring a steady balance of compute power and memory without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. Suitable workloads include enterprise application hosting, web serving, containerized microservices, and moderate database management systems.
description: Amazon Web Services m8ine.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an x86_64 Intel processor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs across 16 physical cores, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of system memory. This configuration maintains a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance does not include local storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage, but it provides a high baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. Lacking a dedicated GPU, the instance is optimized for network and EBS performance. It offers a cost-efficient profile for general-purpose workloads by balancing compute and memory resources without the added expense of specialized hardware. The m8ine.8xlarge is suited for enterprise web serving, application hosting, containerized microservices, and moderate database deployments.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8ine.8xlarge is an x86_64 Intel-based general-purpose instance with 32 vCPUs, 128.0 GB RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for balanced workloads and EBS storage.

tagline: A balanced x86_64 compute platform featuring dedicated Intel processors, high network throughput, and a standard memory-to-core ratio for general-purpose workloads.

128.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
